seoTitle: Connect WhatsApp to n8n: Link, Use & Trigger Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to connect WhatsApp Business Cloud API to n8n, send templated messages, and trigger workflows from incoming chats.
intro: Connecting WhatsApp to n8n turns your automation canvas into a two-way messaging hub, letting you send templated notifications and react to incoming customer replies. This guide covers setting up the WhatsApp Business Cloud API node, configuring your access credentials, and building both outbound messages and inbound triggers.

steps.heading: Start a new workflow
steps.body: Open a fresh, empty canvas in your n8n workspace to build the WhatsApp integration.

steps.heading: Add the WhatsApp Business Cloud node
steps.body: Search for and deploy the official WhatsApp node onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Set up your credentials
steps.body: Enter your system access token and phone number ID in the credential submenu to authenticate the connection.

steps.heading: Configure your message type
steps.body: Choose between a pre-approved message template or a raw free-text message, then map any dynamic fields into the body.

steps.heading: Add a WhatsApp trigger for replies
steps.body: Set up a dedicated trigger node to listen for and route incoming customer messages into your workflow.

steps.heading: Test with a real number
steps.body: Run a live test to confirm outbound delivery and inbound trigger both work before scaling to production.

faq.question: Do I need a WhatsApp Business account to use this integration?
faq.answer: Yes, you need access to the WhatsApp Business Cloud API, which requires setting up a business account through Meta.

faq.question: Can I send free-form messages to any customer?
faq.answer: Generally you can only send free-form replies within a limited response window after a customer messages you first; otherwise you need an approved message template.

faq.question: How do I trigger a workflow from an incoming WhatsApp message?
faq.answer: Set up a dedicated WhatsApp trigger node with a webhook listener that fires whenever a new customer message arrives.

faq.question: Should I test with my own number before going live?
faq.answer: Yes, testing delivery paths with individual test numbers first helps catch configuration issues before you run scaled broadcasts.
